seoTitle: Share Your Screen in Teams and See Participants Too
metaDescription: Present in Microsoft Teams without losing sight of your audience by using Presenter Mode and window sharing to keep faces and chat visible.
intro: Sharing your entire desktop in Teams can hide the participant gallery right when you most need to see raised hands or read reactions. This guide explains how to share just a single window and use Presenter Mode so you stay visually connected to your audience while presenting.

steps.heading: Share a window instead of your whole desktop
steps.body: Choose to share a specific application window rather than your full Desktop, which keeps the main Teams gallery panel accessible on your screen.

steps.heading: Choose a Presenter Mode layout
steps.body: Open the Presenter Mode options and pick Standout, Side-by-side, or Reporter to blend your webcam feed with your shared content.

steps.heading: Adjust your mini-preview view
steps.body: Use the Edit Your View option on the minimized presenter dock to resize elements and reposition your video relative to your content.

steps.heading: Reposition the participant gallery
steps.body: Use the View menu to move the participant thumbnail row to the top of your screen so you can track reactions while presenting.

steps.heading: Use PowerPoint Live for built-in visibility
steps.body: If you're presenting slides, choose the PowerPoint Live option from the share tray to see speaker notes, chat, and participant video simultaneously.

faq.question: Why can't I see participants when I share my whole desktop?
faq.answer: Sharing the full desktop hides the Teams window behind your other apps — sharing a single window instead keeps the gallery visible.

faq.question: What's the difference between Standout and Side-by-side presenter modes?
faq.answer: Standout blends your video into the content as an overlay, while Side-by-side places your webcam feed next to your shared screen.

faq.question: Can I see the chat while presenting?
faq.answer: Yes, PowerPoint Live and the repositioned participant gallery both let you monitor chat and reactions during a presentation.

faq.question: Does this work on a single-monitor setup?
faq.answer: Yes, sharing a window and using the mini-dock preview is designed to work even without a second monitor.
